label: Arb Scanner
description: Live odds grid and arb opportunity stream with minimum arb threshold filter and one-click trade.

0.5.note: No direct mock imports in widget file.

0.6.note: No async loading path (synchronous data, no isLoading surface).

0.7.note: Empty state handled by ArbGrid and ArbStream sub-components.

1.1.note: Data via useSportsData() — no inline fetch.

1.2.note: Context calls useGlobalScope() and uses organizationIds.

1.3.note: Org filter gates bets (hasSportsDesk); fixtures are venue-wide public events, not org-scoped.

1.4.note: Sports fixtures have no client-level scope.

1.5.note: Strategy filter not applicable to sports.

1.7.note: ARB_THRESHOLD_OPTIONS/DEFAULT_ARB_THRESHOLD remain in lib/mocks/fixtures/sports-fixtures (config constants — acceptable scope).

l2.notes: Covered globally by commits 32ba8ab and f9f29ea. Re-audit per-widget only if the wiring test fails or entitlements are added.

3.1.note: Mode relevance: live ✓ (real-time arb scanning), paper ✓ (arb visible, no real trade submission), batch ✗ (arb scanning is live-only — in batch mode context filters to completed fixtures, no live odds available).

3.2.note: Context reads useExecutionMode() → isBatch; filteredFixtures in batch mode only shows completed fixtures (no live arb opportunities).

3.5.note: Paper mode: arb scanner shows opportunities; trade action expected to place simulated bet. Actual paper trade behavior is in ArbTab sub-component.

3.6.note: Widget itself is thin (15 lines) — delegates to ArbTab. No direct WS or interval. WS managed by context.

l3.notes: Agent checks 3.1/3.2/3.5/3.6 pass. Human checks 3.3/3.4 pending.

4.1.note: Widget passes arbThreshold and setArbThreshold to ArbTab — no dead buttons at widget level. ArbTab sub-component handles button logic.

4.2.note: No href='#' or dead links at widget level.

l4.notes: Agent checks pass. Human checks 4.3/4.4/4.7/4.8 pending: verify threshold slider works, arb opportunities clickable, trade fires correctly.

5.1.note: Target strategies: sports-arb. This widget is the primary interface for sports arbitrage opportunity identification and execution.

l5.notes: Agent check 5.1 complete. Human walkthrough 5.2-5.6 pending.

l6.notes: L6 deferred — tests not yet written for sports domain.

7.1.note: No hardcoded hex/rgb/hsl colors found — widget is 15 lines, only tailwind design tokens used.

7.2.note: No mock imports in widget file.

7.5.note: Out of scope — English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21; 7.5 reassigned to UTC datetime rendering

7.7.note: No useMemo/useEffect in this widget file.

l7.notes: All checks pass. Widget is a thin delegation wrapper — audit depth limited by simplicity.

8.1.note: No text-[] or fontSize arbitrary values.

8.3.note: No custom div primitives — delegates to ArbTab component.

8.4.note: Agent: no img/input/div-onClick issues in widget file. Human: keyboard walk of ArbTab pending.

8.5.note: Human-only: resize test at minW=4/minH=4 pending.

keepSeparate.rationale: Arb scanner is a standalone tool. Shares fixture data with sports-fixtures via context but neither drives nor depends on it for selection.

relationships.notes: Consumes filteredFixtures from SportsDataContext (same feed as sports-fixtures). Independent UX flow.

dataContract.notes: Arb opportunities require real-time odds feeds from multiple bookmakers. In production: WebSocket per bookmaker or dedicated arb aggregator service.

gaps: ArbTab sub-component not fully audited in this pass
Multi-bookmaker arb detection logic not verified end-to-end
coverage.notes: Widget delegates all arb logic to ArbTab sub-component.

findings.description: sports-arb-widget.tsx — no user-visible strings; i18n N/A for this wrapper
findings.fix: Retired 7.5 i18n check: English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21
